14 serialization and file 1/0
Objects can be flattened and inflated. Objects have state and behavior. 
Behavior lives in the class, but state lives within each individual object. 50 what happens when 
it’5 time to save the state of an object? If you’re writing a game, you’re gonna need a Save/
Restore Game feature. If you’re writing an app that creates charts, you’re gonna need a Save/
Open File feature. If your program needs to save state, you can do it the hard way, interrogating 
each object, then painstakingly writing the value of each instance variable to a file, in a 
format you create. Or, you can do it the easy 00 way—you simply freeze-dry/flatten/persist/
dehydrate the object itself, and reconstitute/inflate/restore/rehydrate it to get it back. But you’11 
still have to do it the hard way sometimes, especially when the file your app saves has to be read 
by some other non-Java application, 50 we’11 look at both in this chapter.

data, 1 think 1’11 have to kill him. He 
knows 1 can save whole objects, but 
does he let me? NO, that would be 
too easy. Well, we’11 just see how 
he feels after 1...
Saving Objects
